D5 Design L5 Evaluate Reference

Cubehelix

a colour helix wound around the grey diagonal — luminance always climbs, so it prints perfectly in black and white

Dave Green's cubehelix spirals through RGB space around the black-to-white diagonal. By construction the perceived lightness increases monotonically with the data value, so the colours add hue and saturation without ever breaking the greyscale ordering — ideal when a figure might be photocopied.

Tune the start hue, number of rotations, hue amplitude, and gamma. The colour ramp changes character, but the greyscale ramp below it always stays a smooth black-to-white — its luminance never dips.
